methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (36-vote ISA.28.16↔PSA.118.22, 27-vote ISA.28.16→ACT.4.11) + patristic commentary reading (13 entries on Is 28:16: Augustine, Jerome, Cyril of Jerusalem, Eusebius, Gregory of Nyssa, Peter Chrysologus; 8 entries on Eph 2:20: Chrysostom quadruple unity) + dual nature mapping (foundation vs stumbling) + topic verification (CORNERSTONE, FOUNDATION, STUMBLING, ROCK)

evidence: commentaries (Augustine, Haydock — cites Is+Mt+Act+1Pe in one comment)
commentaries (13 entries! Augustine 'two walls', Jerome cites 1Pe+Mt+Rm, Cyril of Jerusalem, Eusebius 'Moses+Gospel', Gregory of Nyssa Gn28+Dn2, Peter Chrysologus)
commentaries (Chrysostom 'builders = teachers of Israel', Haydock, Theophylact)
commentaries (Chrysostom 'quadruple unity: Jews+Gentiles+Apostles+Prophets', Ambrosiaster, Clement, Tertullian, Victorinus — 8 entries)

new_refs_added.justification: 22 votes para ISA.28.16. 9 votes para ROM.9.33. Repeats Psalm 118:22 and completes the positive/negative pair with 2:8. 'For you who believe, it is precious; but for the disobedient, the stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.'

15 votes para ISA.28.16. 9 votes para 1PE.2.6-8. Ambrosiaster connects to Daniel 2:34 and 1 Corinthians 10:4. The NEGATIVE face of the metaphor: 'a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense.' Essential for testing whether search retrieves BOTH sides of the dual nature.

candidates_noted.justification: 22 votes para ISA.28.16. 7 votes para ISA.8.14. Completes the triad 2:6-7-8. 'A stone of stumbling and a rock of offense.'
17 votes de PSA.118.22. Synoptic parallel to Mt 21:42.
3 votes de MAT.21.44. Ambrosiaster cites in Rm 9:33. Gregory of Nyssa cites in Is 28:16. Eschatological dimension: stone cut without hands.

dual_nature_of_the_stone.description: The SAME stone produces opposite effects depending on the response (faith or rejection)

positive_face.name: Foundation/Cornerstone
positive_face.effect: 'Whoever believes in it will not be put to shame'
positive_face.function: Unites Jews and Gentiles as two walls joined at the corner (Augustine)

negative_face.name: Stumbling Stone
negative_face.effect: 'Whoever falls on it will be broken; on whoever it falls, it will crush'

dual_nature_of_the_stone.significance_for_tcc: A search for 'pedra angular' must retrieve BOTH the foundation texts (Is 28:16, Eph 2:20) AND the stumbling texts (Rm 9:33, 1Pe 2:8). Systems that only match 'angular' miss the stumbling dimension entirely.

chrysostom_quadruple_unity.description: The cornerstone binds FOUR groups: Jews + Gentiles + Apostles + Prophets
chrysostom_quadruple_unity.significance: Goes beyond the common interpretation (two walls = Jews/Gentiles) to include the temporal dimension (Prophets before, Apostles after)

isaiah_28_16_as_hub.note: Central hub of the entire 'cornerstone' network — every other gold ref connects back to this verse. 6 different patristic commentators each bring unique insights.
